testing_centers.notes: In-person test centers worldwide and online proctored via OnVUE

id_requirements: Two forms of ID are required. Primary ID must be government-issued, include candidate's name, photo, and signature (e.g. passport, driver's license). Secondary ID must include candidate's name and signature or name and photo.
exam_rules: No notes, scratch paper, electronic devices, books, or study materials permitted. Personal items must be stored. No food or drink except water. Restroom breaks allowed but timer continues. Testing center staff may monitor via video/audio.
renewal_options: Earn CE credits through CompTIA CE program (training, conferences, publishing, teaching, higher certs) or retake the current exam version before expiry.
target_audience: Anyone exploring a career in tech

retake_policy.notes: No waiting period required before first retake. After the second failed attempt, candidates must wait 14 calendar days before any subsequent attempt. No limit on total attempts.

objectives.title: Computing basics
sub_objectives: input, processing, output, and storage fundamentals

objectives.title: Notational systems
sub_objectives: binary, hexadecimal, decimal, and octal systems

objectives.title: Units of measure
sub_objectives: storage (bit, byte, KB, GB, TB), speed (MHz, GHz), and throughput (bps, Mbps, Gbps)

objectives.title: Troubleshooting methodology
sub_objectives: identifying problems, testing theories, implementing solutions, and documenting findings

objectives.title: Computing devices
sub_objectives: smartphones, tablets, laptops, servers, IoT devices, and gaming consoles

objectives.title: Internal components
sub_objectives: motherboard, CPU, RAM, storage (HDD, SSD, NVMe), NIC, and GPU

objectives.title: Storage types
sub_objectives: volatile vs. non-volatile, local, network, and cloud storage

objectives.title: Peripheral setup
sub_objectives: printers, scanners, monitors, and driver installation

objectives.title: Device interfaces
sub_objectives: USB, HDMI, Ethernet, Bluetooth, and NFC

objectives.title: Virtualization and cloud
sub_objectives: hypervisors, SaaS, PaaS, IaaS, hybrid, and on-premises models

objectives.title: Networking basics
sub_objectives: LAN vs. WAN, IP/MAC addresses, routers, switches, and firewalls

objectives.title: Wireless networks
sub_objectives: 802.11 standards, speed, and interference considerations

objectives.title: Operating systems
sub_objectives: mobile, desktop, server, embedded systems

objectives.title: OS components
sub_objectives: file systems (NTFS, FAT32), interfaces (GUI, command line), utilities, and drivers

objectives.title: Software types
sub_objectives: productivity tools, collaboration apps, web browsers, and remote support

objectives.title: Web browser features
sub_objectives: private browsing, add-ons, password management, and cache clearing

objectives.title: Artificial intelligence
sub_objectives: chatbots, assistants, and generative AI for content and predictions

objectives.title: Programming languages
sub_objectives: interpreted, compiled, scripting, markup, and assembly

objectives.title: Data types
sub_objectives: char, strings, numbers (integers, floats), and Boolean

objectives.title: Programming concepts
sub_objectives: variables, constants, arrays, functions, and objects

objectives.title: Organizational techniques
sub_objectives: pseudocode, flowcharts, object-oriented methods, branching, looping

objectives.title: Value of data
sub_objectives: data-driven decisions, reporting, and monetization

objectives.title: Database concepts
sub_objectives: relational vs. non-relational, tables, rows, fields, primary/foreign keys

objectives.title: Database use
sub_objectives: queries, reports, scalability, cloud vs. local storage

objectives.title: Backup concepts
sub_objectives: file and system backups, local vs. cloud storage

objectives.title: Security concepts
sub_objectives: confidentiality, integrity, availability, authentication, and authorization

objectives.title: Device security
sub_objectives: anti-malware, firewalls, patching, physical security, safe browsing

objectives.title: Password practices
sub_objectives: length, complexity, privacy, reuse, and password managers

objectives.title: Encryption
sub_objectives: data at rest, data in transit, HTTPS, VPNs, mobile devices

official_study_resources.notes: Official e-learning with performance-based questions and study guides

official_study_resources.title: CertMaster Practice
official_study_resources.notes: Adaptive practice question engine with rationale
